def: adj. & s.m. Possessing skill of hand, dexterous, masterly, skilful, handy; — mechanical; — a skilled hand, a master-hand; — one who does any work, a 'hand'; a craftsman, an artizan; — s.f. Skill of hand, masterliness of execution or performance, dexterity; art, skill, expertness, or smartness (generally); — operation; — handicraft; handiwork, workmanship; — mode of handling or wielding.

adj. & s.m. = hathauṭī, q.v. adj. & s.m. ety: redupl. conj. part. of haṭhnā, q.v.
def: adv. = haṭ-haṭ-ke, q.v. adv. With great pertinacity; most persistently, or obstinately, &c.; again and again, repeatedly.
s.m. An elephant; — (at chess) the castle or rook; — name of the thirteenth Nakshatra or lunar mansion
subs: s.m. Elephantiasis (syn. fīl-pā); — fossil remains of elephants
s.m. Elephant's house or stable, elephant-shed
adj. (f. -sī), Elephant-like, big as an elephant, huge, bulky
s.f. The galaxy or milky way (syn. ākāś-gaṅgā)
s.f. A small cannon carried on an elephant
(dialec.) hāthi-wān, s.m. Elephant- driver; — name of the thirteenth Nakshatra
s.m. Remission of rent, &c. for losses arising from depredations by wild elephants
s.m. The elephant which leads a procession.

def: adj. & s.m. Pertinacious; importunate; obstinate, &c. (i.q. haṭhīlā, q.v.); — a pertinacious (or an obstinate) person, &c.; a cross or peevish child, &c.
s.f. The plant Pistia stratiotes.
s.m. A kind of grass (it grows about a foot high, and is given as fodder to cattle); — (corr. fr. the English), the globe artichoke, Cynara scolymus.

def: s.m. (dialec. H.) corr. of A. احوال aḥwāl, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

def: The ninth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the sixth of the Arabic, whence it is taken), called ḥāʼě-muhmala (q.v.) and ḥāʼě-ḥut̤t̤ī, 'the ḥā which occurs in the word ḥut̤ī of the abjad.' Its sound is an aspiration much stronger, and formed deeper in the fauces, than that of h. It occurs only in Arabic words, or in secondary formations from Arabic words. In the abjad (q.v.) it has the numerical value eight; in astronomical tables it denotes the sign Sagittarius; and in royal grants, &c. it is the initial letter of the words ḥuẓūr-navīs, 'the Secretary in Waiting,' whose business it was to issue such grants with his siguature affixed.

def: s.m. = هچرمچر hićar-mićar, q.v. s.m. Drawing or hanging back, hesitation, wavering; — excuse; evasion, shuffing; — dispute, cavil
subs: v.t. To draw back, to flinch; — to hesitate, waver; — to loiter, tarry; — to make excuses; to practise evasion, to shuffle; — to cavil.

ety: by redupl. of hićar = hić(nā), q.v.+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः
def: s.m. Drawing or hanging back, hesitation, wavering; — excuse; evasion, shuffing; — dispute, cavil
subs: v.t. To draw back, to flinch; — to hesitate, waver; — to loiter, tarry; — to make excuses; to practise evasion, to shuffle; — to cavil.

def: s.m. A jerk; twitch; jog, jolt; shock, concussion (cf. dhaćkā); a sudden blow or propulsion; — a sudden stoppage (of any business, or affair, &c.); stop, obstruction; a hitch.
v.t. To seize and take (from) violently; to pluck; to snatch (from); — to pull or jerk up or away; — to jerk; to jolt.
adj. Of no use, useless, good for nothing, bad.

ety: hićak˚, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका; — cf. S. हिक्का
def: s.f. The hiccough; convulsive sobbing or breathing; choking (in crying)
subs: (-ko), To breathe spasmodically (as in the agony of death); to be at the point of death, to be dying
(or hićkī) lenā, To hiccough; — to sob bitterly.
wety: Derived from the verb ہِچَکْنَا (hicaknā), which is from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀳𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘𑁆 (*hicc).

ety: hićkić(ānā)+Prk. एअव्व+अट्टं, or + अइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं, or + ता+ इका
def: s.f. Hesitation, hesitancy, doubt; wavering, vacillation; — wriggling
subs: v.n. To take to wavering, &c.; — to gnash the teeth.

ety: hićkićā˚, by redupl. of khic˚ ( = khiṅć˚ = kheṅć, rt. of kheṅćnā, q.v.) + ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā
def: v.n. To draw back; to waver, hesitate; to doubt, be in suspense; to falter; to hum and haw; — to wriggle; to hitch.

ety: hićkić(ānā)+Prk. एअव्व+अट्टं, or + अइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं, or + ता+ इका
def: s.f. Hesitation, hesitancy, doubt; wavering, vacillation; — wriggling
subs: v.n. To take to wavering, &c.; — to gnash the teeth.

def: adj. Having hiccough; hiccoughing; — choking (from crying); sobbing or crying convulsively.

ety: hićkiyā˚ = hićkī+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā
def: v.n. To hiccough.
s.m. A jerk, jolt, shake, shock, &c. (i.q. haćkā).
v.n. (dialec.) = hićkićānā, q.v. v.n. To draw back; to waver, hesitate; to doubt, be in suspense; to falter; to hum and haw; — to wriggle; to hitch.

v.n. = hićaknā, q.v. v.n. To draw back (from, -se), to shrink (from), to stop, to hesitate, to boggle, to waver; to turn (from) in loathing or aversion; to decline; — to jerk, to move by jerks, to hitch, to hobble; to wriggle, twist (cf. khisaknā).

ety: for A. هداية, inf. n. of هدي 'to guide, direct'
def: s.f. Leading one into the right way (in a literal or religious sense); guidance, direction; — injunction, precept; — taking the right path; — righteousness
subs: v.t. To direct with urgency, to enjoin strictly
v.t. To guide, direct; — to instruct, enjoin, charge
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هِدَایَت (hidāyat), borrowed from Arabic هِدَايَة (hidāya), from هَدَى (hadā). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi ہدایت (hdayt /⁠hidāyat⁠/).

def: s.f. Sharpness (of a sword, &c.); the edge (of a sword, &c.); sharpness, keenness, acuteness, acrimony, virulence, poignancy, vehemence, impetuosity, force, strength.
adj. (f. -ī), Soft, limber.
v.n. (dialec.) To become melted, or soft, or flaccid; to lose firmness and soundness.
s.m. Name of a gigantic Rākshas (slain by Bhīm)
subs: or hiḍimb-jit, or hiḍimb-nisūdan, s.m. Epithets of Bhīm (as the destroyer or the conqueror of Hiḍimb)
s.m. 'The killing of Hiḍimb,' title of an episode of the Mahā-bhārat.

def: s.f. Name of the sister of Hiḍimb (she changed herself into a beautiful woman and married Bhīm, by whom she had a son named Ghaṭotkać); — name of the wife of Hanumat
subs: or hiḍimbā-ramaṇ, s.m. The lord, or the husband, of Hiḍimbā; names of Bhīm.

def: v.t. (dialec. Mār.) = hernā, q.v. v.t. To see, observe; to look closely into; to spy out; to peer or pry into; — to look after; to search for; to hunt, chase, pursue; — to catch; to stop.

ety: prob. a later, or a Persian, formation, fr. A. ḥifāz̤
def: s.f. Guarding, keeping. preservation, care, custody, guardianship, charge, protection; security, safety; — memory (cf. ḥifz̤)
subs: (-kī), To take care (of), to keep, preserve, guard, defend; to keep in custody, to secure; — to be careful, be on (one's) guard; — to keep in memory; to commit to, or repeat from, memory
(-ko), To place in security, or in custody, to preserve, to guard, &c. ( = ḥifāz̤at karnā)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حِفاظَت, from Arabic حِفَاظ (ḥifāẓ). Related to حافظ (hāfiz).

def: s.m. Preservation, keeping, care, custody, guardianship, protection; — memory; — adv. From memory; by heart
subs: (-ko), To repeat from memory, to say by heart
s.m. 'Attention to what is before (one)'; foresight, precaution
s.m. Attention to degrees or ranks (of persons), observing etiquette; etiquette.

def: intj. Expressive of surprise, merriment, or laughter; — hee hee! — s.f. Giggling; giggle.
ex: مَیں ہی یہ کام خَتَم کَرُوں گا۔
ma͠i hī ye kām xatam karū̃ gā.
Only I will finish this work.
مَیں یہ کام خَتَم ہی کَرُوں گا۔
ma͠i ye kām xatam hī karū̃ gā.
I will definitely finish this work.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit हि (hi, “indeed, surely, of course, certainly”, emphatic particle).

def: v.n. A dialec. form of hinhinānā, q.v.
s.m. A veil; a curtain; — concealment; — modesty, bashfulness, shame; — night
subs: v.n. A veil, or a curtain, to be raised or removed; the sense of shame to be lost
v.t. To veil, cover, hide, conceal; — to exhibit a sense of shame or modesty; — ḥijāb khānā, v.n. To feel shame, be bashful; to blush.

def: adj. Veiled; modest; — s.f. Concealment, secrecy.

(fr. حجز 'to hinder,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'A thing intervening, a barrier or obstacle'; Mecca and the adjacent country, Arabia Petræa; — one of the three principal musical modes of the Persians (the other two being isfahān and ʻirāq).

def: s.m. Spelling
subs: To syllable, to spell; — (met.) to drive into a corner; — to probe (a matter)
(-ke ?), To pull to pieces; to find fault (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هِجَاء (hijā'), from Arabic هِجَاء (hijāʔ)

def: s.m. = hijrat, q.v. s.f. Separation (of lovers or friends); departure (from one's country and friends); — emigration to Mecca, or from a country of infidels to a land of Muslims; — the Hijra (vulg. 'Hegira'), or flight of Muḥammad from Mecca to Medina (which happened on the 16th of July, A.D. 622, and, in the reign of the Caliph Omar, was ordered to be considered as the commencement of the Muḥammadan era)

s.m. = هيجڙا hījṛā, q.v. s.m. = hijrat, q.v. s.f. 'Hegira'), or flight of Muḥammad from Mecca to Medina (which happened on the 16th of July, A.D. 622, and, in the reign of the Caliph Omar, was ordered to be considered as the commencement of the Muḥammadan era)
s.m. A eunuch; — a hermaphrodite (i.q. hijṛā); — adj. Unmanly; pusillanimous, cowardly.

ety: inf. n. of هجر 'to be separated (from
def: s.m. Being separated (from); — separation, &c. (i.q. hajr, q.v.)
subs: part. adj. & s.m. Separated (from), remote (from); deserted, forsaken; — one who is separated, &c.

ety: for A. هجرة, v.n. fr. هجر; see hajr
def: s.f. Separation (of lovers or friends); departure (from one's country and friends); — emigration to Mecca, or from a country of infidels to a land of Muslims; — the Hijra (vulg. 'Hegira'), or flight of Muḥammad from Mecca to Medina (which happened on the 16th of July, A.D. 622, and, in the reign of the Caliph Omar, was ordered to be considered as the commencement of the Muḥammadan era)
adj. Of or relating to the Hijra or Muḥammadan era; according to the Muḥammadan era.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هجری (hijrī), from Arabic هِجْرِيّ (hijriyy, “of or relating to the Hijra/Hegira”).

def: s.f. Sickness at stomach, nausea, qualm; — disgust; — a bad smell; a bad taste or flavour (perhaps, as causing nausea or disgust)
subs: (-se), or hīk mārnā, To smell offensive.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit हिक्का (hikkā). Doublet of ہِکَّا (hikkā), a tatsama.

def: s.f. Wisdom, knowledge, science, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُکْم (hukm), borrowed from Arabic حُكْم (ḥukm).

ety: for A. حکاية, inf. n. of حکي 'to relate,' &c.
def: s.f. Narrative, narration, history, story, tale, romance
subs: To relate, recount, to tell; — to argue, dispute.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حِکَایَت (hikāyat), from Arabic حِكَايَة (ḥikāya).

def: s.f. pl. (of ḥikāyat), Tales, stories, &c.
(dialec.), contrac. of hiʼa-ke = hiye-ke = hr̤iday-ke, gen. of hr̤iday.

wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit हिक्का (hikkā). Doublet of ہِیک (hīk).

ety: for A. حکمة, v.n. fr. حکم
def: s.f. Wisdom; knowledge, science; philosphy; — mystery; miracle; — cleverness, skill, art, contrivance, ingenuity, device, address; management, frugality, economy; — the functions of a physician

To exercise wisdom or skill, &c.; to contrive, scheme, plot; — to practise physic.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حکمت (hikmat), from Arabic حِكْمَة (ḥikma).

def: adj. Of wisdom; knowing; philosophical; clever, ingenious, skilful; artful, scheming, dodgy; — of management, frugal, economical.
s.f. Cultivated reeds grown on low marshy ground.
